headline: Agent tooling and the Model Context Protocol dominated the quarter as MCP rotated to the top alongside a cluster of agent-orchestration techniques.
body: MCP led the movers and recurred across multiple new items, from Google Cloud agent tutorials grounding ADK agents in Looker to introductory breakdowns of MCP client, server, and JSON-RPC architecture. Adjacent techniques including token efficiency, scoped skills, subagents, and AI-assisted development moved together, reflected in coverage of running 128 parallel coding agents and local agent stacks that build with frontier models and execute on-device. Google Chrome's proposed WebMCP standard extended the same structured-tool pattern to the open web, replacing DOM automation with named typed actions. Separately, Claude Fable 5 drew backlash over opaque safety filters and uncertainty about whether responses came from the full model or a constrained fallback.
